questions.original_question: 路特斯科技和LVMH的关系是什么？
questions.answer:
路特斯科技（Lotus Technology）与LVMH之间目前没有直接的合作协议。然而，路特斯科技通过与L Catterton的业务合并，间接获得了与LVMH的战略关系支持。L Catterton与LVMH有着独家合作关系，这为路特斯科技提供了全球消费者洞察和品牌协作的机会，有助于提升其全球品牌知名度。路特斯科技正在从传统的英国跑车制造商转型为高性能电动汽车的全球先锋，这一转型得到了吉利控股的全面支持。L Catterton与LVMH的关系为路特斯科技提供了接触LVMH高层管理团队、全球消费者洞察和行业网络的机会，但L Catterton在投资和组合管理决策上保持独立。

以上信息基于2024年5月24日之前的知识。

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: Lotus is an innovative luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for creating high-end lifestyle vehicles that blend performance, design, and engineering excellence, backed by over 70 years of racing heritage. Founded in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has transitioned from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, especially after Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in 2017. This partnership has facilitated significant advancements in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and logistics, enabling Lotus to focus on electrification and intelligent automotive solutions.

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow at a CAGR of 35% from 2021 to 2031, is currently underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. Lotus aims to capitalize on this gap by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features an innovative 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture, offering exceptional performance, rapid charging capabilities, and a competitive price point. The company plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with additional models like the Emeya and a D-segment SUV set for future release.

Lotus emphasizes its R&D capabilities as a competitive advantage, leveraging its proprietary technologies and advanced driver assistance systems. The company operates five R&D facilities across the U.K., Germany, and China, fostering collaboration among skilled teams. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which allows for scalability and flexibility while minimizing capital investments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a global digital platform that includes a virtual showroom and customization options. The brand has established a global distribution network, with plans to expand its store count from 215 to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, reflecting its commitment to leading the electrification of luxury vehicles while maintaining strong ties to its British heritage.

title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

title: L CATTERTON ASIA ACQUISITION CORP NOTES TO FINANCIAL STATEMENTS  DECEMBER 31, 2023 
summary: The Company is seeking shareholder approval to amend its memorandum and articles of association, focusing on the redemption rights of Class A ordinary shareholders in connection with an initial Business Combination. The proposed amendments aim to modify the conditions under which shares can be redeemed, particularly if the Business Combination is not completed by the extended liquidation date. Public Shareholders who redeem their shares will not be eligible for funds from the Trust Account upon completion of a Business Combination or liquidation if the Company fails to finalize the transaction within the designated timeframe.

The Company has extended the deadline for completing its initial business combination multiple times, with the current deadline set for March 15, 2024. Financial contributions from the Sponsor will support these extensions, with provisions for additional funding if necessary. Shareholders have the option to redeem their shares for cash based on the Trust Account's total amount, which currently stands at approximately $10.00 per Public Share.

During an Extraordinary General Meeting, a significant number of Class A ordinary shares were redeemed, and the Company has since approved several extensions of its Termination Date, with the latest extending to March 15, 2024. If the Company fails to complete the Business Combination by this date, it will cease operations and redeem Public Shares, adhering to legal requirements.

The Company has also made adjustments to its warrants, which are classified as liabilities and measured at fair value. The valuation of these warrants has been based on market prices and Monte Carlo simulations, with ongoing assessments reflecting changes in fair value. The financial statements include reconciliations of ordinary shares and net income per share, highlighting the performance of Class A and Class B shares over the past two years.

Recent developments include the completion of a Business Combination with Lotus Tech, resulting in a change of control and the Company's subsequent dissolution. The Company has communicated with Nasdaq regarding compliance issues and the delisting of its securities following the Business Combination, which has been officially recorded. Overall, the Company is navigating complex regulatory and financial landscapes while pursuing its strategic objectives.

Founded in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has transitioned from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, especially after Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in 2017. This partnership has facilitated significant advancements in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and logistics, enabling Lotus to focus on electrification and intelligent automotive solutions.

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow at a CAGR of 35% from 2021 to 2031, is currently underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. Lotus aims to capitalize on this gap by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features an innovative 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture, offering exceptional performance, rapid charging capabilities, and a competitive price point. The company plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with additional models like the Emeya and a D-segment SUV set for future release.

Lotus emphasizes its R&D capabilities as a competitive advantage, leveraging its proprietary technologies and advanced driver assistance systems. The company operates five R&D facilities across the U.K., Germany, and China, fostering collaboration among skilled teams. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which allows for scalability and flexibility while minimizing capital investments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a global digital platform that includes a virtual showroom and customization options. The brand has established a global distribution network, with plans to expand its store count from 215 to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, reflecting its commitment to leading the electrification of luxury vehicles while maintaining strong ties to its British heritage.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: According to Oliver Wyman, the global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with MSRP of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly at a CAGR of 35% over 2021-2031 and reach a market size of nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, as compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of its competitors and targeting to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, Eletre, in 2022. Beginning with Eletre, Lotus Technology's new car rollouts will all be BEV models. Lotus Technology expects to take up market share and realize its first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture, or EPA, which is a newly debuted self-developed battery electric vehicle (BEV) platform initially based on the same foundation of Sustainable Experience Architecture, the world’s first open-source BEV architecture. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed.

title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

Lotus is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for its iconic British brand and over 70 years of racing heritage, which includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles. The company specializes in luxury lifestyle vehicles designed for daily use and has been positioned as a luxury lifestyle BEV maker since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017. This acquisition has provided significant support in manufacturing, supply chain management, and research and development.

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is anticipated to experience substantial growth, projected at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, reaching nearly 1.9 million units. Currently, the market is underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. As an early mover in this sector, Lotus aims to launch its E-segment BEV model ahead of competitors and achieve a 100% BEV portfolio by 2027.

The company’s first fully electric model, the Eletre, was launched in 2022 and features an 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture. The Eletre offers three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0-100 km/h acceleration in just 2.95 seconds. Deliveries for the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023, with plans for global expansion.

Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced its second vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Additionally, the company plans to launch two more BEV models: a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. All BEV models are manufactured through a partnership with Geely Holding at a new facility in Wuhan, China, which has an annual production capacity of 150,000 units. This collaboration allows Lotus to scale operations and maintain flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, the company delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and employed 3,180 full-time staff, with 56.7% of the workforce dedicated to research and development. 

This strategic positioning and robust growth plan underscore Lotus's commitment to becoming a leader in the luxury BEV market while leveraging its rich heritage and innovative capabilities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance sports car brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK and further positioned Lotus Technology as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker. Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between the traditional sports car and a new generation of electric vehicles. The proposed business combination with LCAA, a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, is expected to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable Lotus Technology to effectively raise its brand awareness globally.

title: Overview 
summary: LCAA, a blank check company established in the Cayman Islands on January 5, 2021, aims to complete a business combination through mergers or acquisitions. After exploring various opportunities, LCAA signed an Original Merger Agreement on January 31, 2023, which was later amended on October 11, 2023. The merger will be financed using cash from its IPO, proceeds from the sale of LCAA Private Warrants, and possibly PIPE financing.

The merger involves Lotus, a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer with a rich heritage of over 70 years in racing and a reputation for high-performance engineering. Acquired by Geely Holding in 2017, Lotus is positioned to leverage Geely's support in manufacturing and R&D to become a leader in the luxury BEV market. The global luxury BEV market is projected to grow significantly, with a CAGR of 35% from 2021 to 2031, highlighting an opportunity for Lotus to capture market share by launching its E-segment BEV models, starting with the Hyper-SUV Eletre, introduced in 2022.

The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture, offering impressive performance metrics, including a 0-100 km/h acceleration time of 2.95 seconds and a WLTP range of 490 km. With over 8,600 units ordered as of September 30, 2023, and plans for further launches, including the Emeya and additional BEV models, Lotus aims to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027.

Lotus's competitive edge lies in its robust R&D capabilities, rooted in its sports car design heritage, and its proprietary electric powertrain technology. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely, utilizing advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which enhances scalability and supply chain management. The company also emphasizes a luxury retail experience through a digital-first sales model, aiming to expand its global presence from 206 stores to over 300 by 2025. As of September 30, 2023, Lotus has shipped over 4,800 vehicles and holds a cumulative order book of over 19,000 units, positioning itself strongly in the evolving luxury BEV market.

After exploring various opportunities, LCAA signed an Original Merger Agreement on January 31, 2023, which was later amended on October 11, 2023. The merger aims to finance the business combination through cash from its IPO, proceeds from the sale of LCAA Private Warrants, and PIPE financing.

Geely's support in manufacturing, research and development, and logistics has been pivotal in Lotus's evolution into a leader in high-performance electric vehicles.

The luxury BEV market is projected to grow significantly, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, highlighting an underserved segment with limited consumer options. Lotus aims to capture this market by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Eletre, which features advanced technology, exceptional performance, and a commitment to a fully electric product lineup by 2027. The Eletre, priced above $100,000, has already received over 8,600 orders, with deliveries commencing in China and Europe, and plans for U.S. distribution.

Lotus emphasizes its competitive advantage through robust research and development capabilities, including a proprietary 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture and an advanced driver assistance system. The company collaborates with Geely for manufacturing, leveraging its facilities in Wuhan, China, to achieve scalability while minimizing capital investments. 

Additionally, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model to enhance customer experience, with plans to expand its retail presence from 206 stores to over 300 by 2025. As of September 30, 2023, the company has shipped over 4,800 vehicles and has a cumulative global order book exceeding 19,000 units, positioning itself strongly in the growing luxury BEV market.

No shareholder will otherwise have any right to inspect any account, book, or document of Lotus Technology except as required by law or authorized by the board of directors or the shareholders by special resolution. Lotus Technology may from time to time by ordinary resolution: ● increase the share capital by such sum, to be divided into shares of such classes and amount, as the resolution will prescribe; ● consolidate and divide all or any share capital into shares of a larger amount than existing shares; ● sub-divide the existing shares or any of them into shares of a smaller amount; provided that in the subdivision the proportion between the amount paid and the amount, if any, unpaid on each reduced share will be the same as it was in the case of the share from which the reduced share is derived;

title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The chapter on "Inspection of Books and Records" outlines the protocols established by the board of directors regarding shareholder access to the company's financial documents. The board holds the authority to define the conditions under which shareholders may inspect the company's accounts and books, including the extent of access, timing, location, and specific regulations governing such inspections. 

It is important to note that shareholders do not possess an inherent right to inspect company documents. Access is contingent upon either legal mandates, explicit authorization from the board, or approval through a special resolution passed by the shareholders. This framework ensures that the inspection process is regulated and aligned with both corporate governance and legal requirements.

In summary, the board of directors plays a crucial role in determining the parameters for document inspection, thereby safeguarding the company's interests while also adhering to applicable laws that may grant shareholders certain rights.
